message:
fix(session): add fsync durability to session.rs JSONL persistence paths

Extends the crash-safety pattern from session_store_v2.rs (commit
95b7ff0f) to the original session.rs persistence code:

- save_jsonl_full_rewrite_blocking: fsync the temp file contents before
  persist(), then sync_parent_dir after the atomic rename, ensuring
  both file data and the directory entry are durable before returning
- append_jsonl_entries_blocking: fsync after appending serialized
  entries to the session file, preventing data loss if the kernel
  crashes between the write and the next natural sync

Adds the same platform-conditional sync_parent_dir helper (Unix: open
parent dir + sync_all; non-Unix: no-op) used in session_store_v2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
